goal: Give a face-to-point or point-to-point distance in mm between fixture top face and EOAT tip.
thoughts: measure_distance is usually point-to-point in Isaac Sim; for face-to-point, grab the face representative point from get_bounding_box (top-face center = bbox top-center). Return mm to 2 decimals, honest about sim resolution (mesh tessellation + float noise ~0.05-0.1mm).
code:
prims = list_all_prims(path_prefix="/World/Fixture")
fixture_bbox = get_bounding_box(prim_path="/World/Fixture/top_face", units="mm")
top_center = (
    (fixture_bbox.min[0] + fixture_bbox.max[0]) / 2,
    (fixture_bbox.min[1] + fixture_bbox.max[1]) / 2,
    fixture_bbox.max[2]  # top face z
)
dist = measure_distance(
    point_a=top_center,
    prim_b="/World/EOAT/tip",
    units="mm"
)
# Report: f"{dist.value_mm:.2f} mm (+/- <TOL> mm sim resolution)"
failure_modes: Handing her a Python script when measure_distance covers the case
Returning 6-decimal float precision and implying mm-scale accuracy
Using point-to-point when she asked face-to-face without explaining the workaround
